meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: How GateCtr collects, uses, and protects your personal data.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: GateCtr is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains what data we collect, why we collect it, how we use it, and your rights. We built GateCtr on a simple principle: your API keys and your prompts are yours. We never sell your data, never train models on your content, and never share your keys with third parties.

s1.title: 1. Who We Are
s1.body: GateCtr is an LLM middleware gateway. We sit between your application and LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Gemini) to optimize, route, and enforce budgets on your API calls. GateCtr is operated by GateCtr SAS. For privacy inquiries, contact us at privacy@gatectr.com.

item1.label: Service
item1.desc: LLM middleware gateway — routing, budget enforcement, prompt optimization, analytics.

item3.label: Scope
item3.desc: This policy applies to all users of gatectr.com, app.gatectr.com, and the GateCtr API.

s2.title: 2. Data We Collect
s2.body: We collect only what is necessary to operate the service. We do not collect the content of your prompts or LLM responses.

item1.label: Account data
item1.desc: Email address, name, and authentication data provided when you sign up via Clerk (Google OAuth or email).

item2.label: Usage metadata
item2.desc: Token counts, request counts, model used, provider, latency, cost estimates, and timestamps. Never the content of prompts or responses.

item3.label: Provider keys
item3.desc: Your LLM API keys, stored AES-256 encrypted at rest. We never log, display, or transmit them in plaintext.

item4.label: Technical data
item4.desc: IP address, user agent, browser type, and request timestamps — used for security, abuse prevention, and analytics.

s3.title: 3. How We Use Your Data
s3.body: We use your data exclusively to:
s3.item1: Provide the service: route API calls, enforce budgets, compress prompts, and display analytics.
s3.item2: Send transactional emails: account creation, budget alerts, onboarding completion, and billing events.
s3.item3: Improve reliability: monitor errors, latency, and system health via Sentry and Vercel Analytics.
s3.item4: Comply with legal obligations: respond to lawful requests from authorities when required.

s4.title: 4. Data Retention
s4.body: We retain data for the minimum period necessary to provide the service.

s5.title: 5. Third-Party Services
s5.body: We use the following sub-processors to operate GateCtr. Each processes data under their own privacy policy and DPA.
s5.clerk: Authentication and session management. Processes email and OAuth data.
s5.stripe: Payment processing. Processes billing information and invoices.
s5.neon: PostgreSQL database hosting (AWS us-east-1). Stores account and usage data.
s5.upstash: Redis caching. Stores temporary session and rate-limit data.
s5.sentry: Error monitoring. Receives anonymized stack traces and performance data.
s5.vercel: Hosting and edge network. Processes all HTTP requests.
s5.note: We do not share your data with LLM providers. Your API key is used directly from your infrastructure — GateCtr acts as a transparent proxy.

s6.title: 6. Your Rights
s6.body: Depending on your jurisdiction, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
s6.item1: Right of access — request a copy of all data we hold about you.
s6.item2: Right to rectification — correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
s6.item3: Right to erasure — request deletion of your account and associated data.
s6.item4: Right to portability — export your usage data in CSV format from the dashboard.
s6.item5: Right to object — opt out of non-essential data processing.
s6.how: To exercise any of these rights, email privacy@gatectr.com. We will respond within 30 days. Account deletion can also be initiated directly from Settings → Account.

s7.title: 7. Security
s7.body: We implement industry-standard security measures: AES-256 encryption for API keys at rest, TLS 1.3 for all data in transit, multi-tenant isolation at the database level, role-based access control (RBAC) for team accounts, and regular security audits. We do not store LLM prompt content. In the event of a data breach affecting your personal data, we will notify you within 72 hours as required by applicable law.

s8.title: 8. International Transfers
s8.body: GateCtr is hosted on Vercel and Neon infrastructure primarily in the United States (AWS us-east-1). If you are located in the European Economic Area (EEA), your data may be transferred to and processed in the United States. We rely on Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission for such transfers.

s9.title: 9. Changes to This Policy
s9.body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of material changes by email or by displaying a notice in the dashboard. The date at the top of this page reflects the most recent update. Continued use of GateCtr after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

contact.title: Privacy inquiries
contact.body: For questions, requests, or concerns about your personal data:

meta.title: Terms of Service
meta.description: The terms and conditions governing your use of GateCtr.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service govern your access to and use of GateCtr. By creating an account or using the service, you agree to be bound by these terms. If you do not agree, do not use GateCtr. These terms apply to all users, including free and paid plans.

s1.title: 1. The Service
s1.body: GateCtr provides a middleware gateway for LLM API calls. The service includes: request routing to LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Gemini), budget enforcement and alerting, prompt compression via the Context Optimizer, usage analytics and reporting, and webhook notifications. GateCtr acts as a transparent proxy. We do not store the content of your prompts or LLM responses. We are not responsible for the output, accuracy, or availability of any LLM provider.

s2.title: 2. Eligibility
s2.item1: Be at least 18 years old or have the legal capacity to enter into contracts in your jurisdiction.
s2.item2: Provide accurate and complete information when creating your account.
s2.item3: Comply with the terms of service of any LLM provider you connect through GateCtr.

s3.title: 3. Account Responsibilities
s3.item1: Maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and API keys.
s3.item2: All activity that occurs under your account, including usage by team members.
s3.item3: Ensuring your use of GateCtr complies with applicable laws and regulations.
s3.item4: Promptly notifying us at security@gatectr.com if you suspect unauthorized access to your account.

s4.title: 4. Acceptable Use
s4.body: You agree not to use GateCtr to:
s4.item1: Violate any applicable law, regulation, or third-party rights.
s4.item2: Circumvent rate limits, abuse the service, or attempt to gain unauthorized access to our systems.
s4.item3: Resell or sublicense access to GateCtr without our written consent.

s5.title: 5. Billing and Payments
s5.body: GateCtr does not charge for tokens — you pay your LLM provider directly using your own API key. GateCtr charges for access to the platform (routing, optimization, analytics). Paid plans are billed monthly or annually via Stripe. Prices are displayed inclusive of all applicable taxes. Upgrades take effect immediately. Downgrades and cancellations take effect at the end of the current billing period. We do not offer refunds except where required by law or at our sole discretion. For billing disputes, contact billing@gatectr.com within 30 days of the charge.

s6.title: 6. Intellectual Property
s6.body: GateCtr and its content, features, and functionality are owned by GateCtr SAS and are protected by intellectual property laws. You retain all rights to your data, prompts, and API keys. By using GateCtr, you grant us a limited license to process your data solely to provide the service.
s6.item1: You may not copy, modify, or distribute GateCtr's software without our written consent.
s6.item2: You may not reverse-engineer or attempt to extract the source code of GateCtr.
s6.item3: The GateCtr name, logo, and trademarks may not be used without our prior written permission.

s7.title: 7. Availability and SLA
s7.body: We target 99.9% uptime for paid plans (Pro, Team, Enterprise). We do not guarantee uninterrupted service. Scheduled maintenance will be announced at least 24 hours in advance via status.gatectr.com. We are not liable for losses caused by service interruptions, including unexpected LLM costs incurred during downtime.

s8.title: 8. Limitation of Liability
s8.body: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, GateCtr SAS shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including but not limited to: unexpected LLM provider costs, loss of data, loss of revenue, or business interruption. Our total liability to you for any claim arising from these terms or your use of the service shall not exceed the amount you paid to GateCtr in the 12 months preceding the claim.

s9.title: 9. Termination
s9.body: You may terminate your account at any time from Settings → Account. We may suspend or terminate your account if you violate these terms, engage in fraudulent activity, or fail to pay amounts owed. Upon termination, your data will be retained for 30 days before deletion, during which you may export it from the dashboard.

s10.title: 10. Governing Law
s10.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, United States, without regard to its conflict of law provisions. Any disputes arising from these terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal and state courts located in Delaware, United States, unless otherwise required by applicable consumer protection law in your jurisdiction.

contact.title: Legal inquiries
contact.body: For questions about these Terms of Service:

meta.title: Cookie Policy
meta.description: How GateCtr uses cookies and similar technologies.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: GateCtr uses cookies and similar technologies to keep you signed in, remember your preferences, and understand how the service is used. This policy explains what cookies we use, why, and how you can control them.

s1.title: 1. What Are Cookies
s1.body: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They allow the website to recognize your device on subsequent visits. GateCtr uses both session cookies (deleted when you close your browser) and persistent cookies (stored for a defined period). We also use localStorage for client-side preference storage.

s2.title: 2. Cookies We Use
s2.body: The following cookies are set when you use GateCtr:

row1.name: __session
row1.purpose: Clerk authentication session token. Required to keep you signed in.

row2.name: __client_uat
row2.purpose: Clerk client update token. Ensures session consistency across tabs.

row3.name: NEXT_LOCALE
row3.purpose: Remembers your language preference (English or French).

row4.name: _vercel_analytics
row4.purpose: Anonymous usage analytics collected by Vercel. No personal data.

row5.name: stripe_*
row5.purpose: Set by Stripe during checkout to prevent fraud and ensure payment security.

s3.title: 3. Third-Party Cookies
s3.body: Clerk sets authentication cookies scoped to app.gatectr.com. These cookies are required for the service to function and cannot be disabled without preventing sign-in. Stripe may set cookies during the checkout process for fraud prevention. We do not use advertising cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party analytics that identify you personally.

s4.title: 4. Managing Cookies
s4.body: You can control cookies through your browser settings. Here is how to manage cookies in the most common browsers:

chrome.label: Chrome
chrome.desc: Settings → Privacy and security → Cookies and other site data.

firefox.label: Firefox
firefox.desc: Settings → Privacy & Security → Cookies and Site Data.

edge.label: Edge
edge.desc: Settings → Cookies and site permissions → Cookies and site data.

s4.warning: Disabling essential cookies (Clerk session cookies) will prevent you from signing in to GateCtr. Disabling functional cookies will reset your language and theme preferences on each visit.

s5.title: 5. Changes to This Policy
s5.body: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. We will notify you of material changes by email or by displaying a notice in the dashboard. The date at the top of this page reflects the most recent update.

contact.title: Cookie inquiries
contact.body: For questions about our use of cookies:
